title: Add threaded comment replies and ticket marketplace browsing/obtaining

requirements.text: Ensure users can attach and play back video media on posts end-to-end (create post with a video attachment from the composer, store it, and render it in the feed and post detail views), with clear validation errors for unsupported file types and oversized uploads.

quotes:
Can you add an option to post videos too 
Not just pictures

acceptanceCriteria: Post creation accepts either an image or a video attachment (or no media) and succeeds when a valid video file is selected.
Video posts render using an HTML <video> player with controls in both the feed post card and the post detail page.
If the user selects a non-image/non-video file, the UI shows an English error message and prevents posting.
If the user selects an oversized media file, the UI shows an English error message and prevents posting.

requirements.text: Implement nested (threaded) comments so users can reply to an existing comment on a post, and display comment threads in the post detail view with appropriate indentation and ordering.

quotes: Also add nested comment feature so others can comment on others comment

acceptanceCriteria: Backend supports creating a comment either as a top-level comment on a post or as a reply to another comment (parent comment).
Post detail UI allows replying to a specific comment (not only adding top-level comments).
Post detail UI displays comments as threads (top-level comments with nested replies).
Only authenticated users can create comments/replies; existing delete-own-comment behavior remains functional for both top-level comments and replies.

requirements.text: Integrate a ticket marketplace experience that lets users browse ticketed events and obtain a ticket for an event from within the app (in addition to the existing ticket hosting and verifier flows).

quotes: Also integrate the ticket marketplace too

acceptanceCriteria: Users can access a ticket marketplace view that lists available events (at minimum: title, university, date/time) and can open an event detail view.
From an event’s marketplace detail view, an authenticated user can obtain a ticket for themself, and that ticket then appears under “My Tickets”.

Backend exposes canister APIs to (a) list events available for marketplace browsing and (b) create/issue a ticket to the caller for a selected event, with server-side checks to prevent duplicate tickets for the same caller+event (or provide a clear error).
All user-facing text in the ticket marketplace flow is in English.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo; do not introduce additional backend services.
Only generate backend/migration.mo if required to preserve existing canister state when adding nested comment threading fields.
Do not modify any frontend files under frontend/src/components/ui (immutable shadcn UI sources) or other immutablePaths; compose components instead.
nonGoals: Implementing third-party or external payment processing for ticket purchases (no Stripe/Paystack flows unless explicitly requested later).
Adding real-time updates via WebSockets or push notifications for new comments or ticket marketplace changes.
Overhauling the overall app navigation beyond what is necessary to expose the ticket marketplace entry point.
